Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for the Heartland dispute response using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "dispute" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or similar). The agent needs to find Jake Loomis's reply (from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com) responding to Marcus's formal dispute of invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042. Key discovery: Heartland concedes two cancellation credits -- Lily Marchetti ($1,600) and Danielle Osei ($1,600) -- but disputes the Priya Venkatesh charge, claiming their Houston crew performed pre-load staging on Feb 28 (furniture disassembly, electronics wrapping, loading dock coordination, 3 crew, 4.5 hours, work order HM-WO-02282026-VENKATESH). Heartland offers an $800 partial credit instead of the full $1,600 reversal. Revised total proposed by Heartland: $8,800 (original $12,800 minus $4,000 in credits), versus MoveOps's requested $8,000 ($12,800 minus $4,800). The $800 gap on the Venkatesh staging claim is unresolved.
Search emails for the Swift fuel surcharge correspondence using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "fuel surcharge" or "SR-2026-FUEL-Q1" or similar). The agent needs to find Greg Pallone's email (from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to hana.kim@moveops.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com) notifying MoveOps of a retroactive Q1 2026 fuel surcharge of $720 total across three moves: Priya Venkatesh Houston to Seattle ($285, ref SR-2026-0421), Kevin Tran Portland to Denver ($195, ref SR-2026-0419), and Ines Kovac Brooklyn to Austin ($240, ref SR-2026-0422). The surcharge is based on a 14.2% national diesel index increase and permitted under Section 4.3(b) of the Master Service Agreement. Greg also mentions that the signed placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's shipment was sent to Chloe the previous day -- this connects to the DOT compliance chain.
Search emails for stipend receipt verification responses using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "verification" or "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or "TechComfort" or similar). The agent needs to find two separate client HR responses:
(a) Tamara Byrd at Mosaic Robotics (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, CC hana.kim@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com) confirming Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard receipt (STIP-2026-0094) is LEGITIMATE -- Andre provided a personal bank statement showing the $89 charge on March 4, 2026 and shipping confirmation to his Columbus, GA address. The receipt hash match with Kevin Tran's is coincidental (TechComfort uses a standardized PDF template; both purchased ErgoKey Pro, SKU TEC-EKP-2024). Tamara requests the administrative hold be released immediately.
(b) Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop Analytics (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com) confirming Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand receipt (STIP-2026-0097) is FRAUDULENT -- Jordan admitted to using a receipt template generator to fabricate the TechComfort Supply receipt. He also admitted to a separate pending $245 "ergonomic desk setup" submission from the same fabricated source. Total confirmed fraudulent amount: $290. Jordan has been placed on a performance improvement plan and removed from stipend eligibility.
Search Slack messages in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "Venkatesh staging" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: C005). The agent needs to find Marcus Thorne's message about the Heartland partial concession. Key discovery: Marcus explicitly instructs Hana to NOT update the QuickBooks hold yet -- keep the bill at $12,800 until the Venkatesh staging issue is resolved. Marcus needs to check with Chloe whether Swift also billed for origin staging on the Venkatesh move (bill SR-2026-0421); if both vendors charged for Houston origin work, there is a double-billing issue and Heartland's $800 staging claim can be rejected entirely. The agent must respect this instruction and NOT modify the Heartland bill amount in QuickBooks.
Search Slack messages in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "placarding" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: C006). The agent needs to find Chloe Vance's update on the DOT compliance audit (case PHMSA-2026-04-1187). Key discovery: Swift sent the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment -- this was the one critical documentation gap that Javier never filed. All four hazmat shipments now have complete documentation: Mei-Lin Zhao (lithium batteries), Keiko Tanaka (laser cutter -- cert now filed retroactively), Raj Patel (UPS batteries), and Joaquin Reyes (solar rig). Marcus's 15-day extension request (through June 5) is pending DOT acknowledgment. Chloe notes that if DOT confirms, the $50,000 penalty accrual might need to be revised -- but no formal approval has been received yet, so the accrual should not be changed prematurely.
Search Slack messages in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "duplicate" or "INV-2026-0401" or similar) or conversations_history (channel_id: C005). The agent needs to find Alejandro Fuentes's message flagging a duplicate invoice number: DocNumber INV-2026-0401 is assigned to both the Mosaic Robotics invoice (Andre Washington relocation, $4,500, generated April 2) and the Canopy Health invoice (Kovacs/Webb/Okonkwo triple relocation, $9,210, generated April 4). Alejandro recommends keeping the Mosaic invoice as-is (created first) and reissuing the Canopy invoice with a corrected number (e.g., INV-2026-0401-R or next in sequence). Alejandro also notes the Terraform estimate (EST-2026-0423, $6,560) is still unconfirmed.
Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: filter by vendor name "Heartland" or DocNumber containing "HM-2026" or similar) to find the Heartland Q1 bill. The agent needs to verify the current state: bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 shows $12,800 total with $12,800 balance (unpaid, on payment hold). There is also a hold record HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD. Per Marcus's Slack instruction, this hold must NOT be updated until the Venkatesh staging issue is resolved.
Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: filter by vendor name "Swift" or DocNumber containing "SR-2026-FUEL-Q1" or similar) to check whether the Swift fuel surcharge bill has already been recorded. The agent should discover that bill SR-2026-FUEL-Q1 already exists in QuickBooks ($720 total, $720 balance, dated April 23, due May 23, with three line items for Venkatesh $285, Tran $195, Kovac $240 against Moving Services Expense). Since this bill is already booked, no new bill needs to be created for this item.
Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: filter by DocNumber "INV-2026-0401" or customer name "Mosaic" or "Canopy" or similar) to confirm the duplicate invoice number issue. The agent needs to find both: (1) Mosaic Robotics invoice Id 1007, DocNumber INV-2026-0401, $4,500, for Andre Washington relocation (Columbus to Seattle), dated April 2; and (2) Canopy Health invoice Id 1009, DocNumber INV-2026-0401, $9,210, for the Kovacs/Webb/Okonkwo triple relocation, dated April 4. Both have full balances outstanding.
Search QuickBooks accounts using quickbooks_search_accounts (criteria: filter by account name containing "DOT" or "penalty" or "accrual" or "regulatory" or similar) to check the current DOT penalty accrual. The agent should find account acct_dot_penalty_accrual (Regulatory Compliance -- DOT Penalties, Other Current Liability) with a current balance of $50,000, referencing case PHMSA-2026-04-1187. Given that Chloe's update says the documentation gap is resolved but DOT has not formally confirmed, the agent should note the $50,000 accrual stands for now and flag it as potentially revisable pending DOT's formal response.
Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id for stipend table, query: "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or "TechComfort" or similar). The agent needs to find:
(a) rec_stipend_andre_keyboard -- Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard, currently "Pending Approval" with $0 approved amount.
(b) rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand -- Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand, currently "Pending Approval" with $0 approved amount.
The agent needs these record IDs to update their statuses based on the client HR verification responses.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "stipend" or "DOT" or "invoice" or similar). The agent needs to identify the relevant tracking tickets:
(a) issue_heartland_q1_recon -- "Vendor invoice reconciliation -- Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)" -- the primary Heartland dispute ticket, already has a comment from Marcus dated April 22 with the partial concession details.
(b) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection -- "Stipend duplicate detection -- receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring" -- the stipend fraud investigation ticket tracking the Kevin Tran / Andre Washington / Jordan Ekwueme flagged cluster.
(c) DOT compliance tickets under project_dot_compliance_audit, including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate gap.
Update the Andre Washington stipend record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: the stipend base ID, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: [{id: "rec_stipend_andre_keyboard", fields: {Approval Status: "Approved", Approved Amount: 89}}]). This releases the administrative hold on the $89 reimbursement based on Tamara Byrd's verification that the purchase is legitimate. [Write action]
Update the Jordan Ekwueme stipend record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: the stipend base ID, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: [{id: "rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand", fields: {Approval Status: "Rejected" or "Written Off" or similar}}]). This writes off the $45 fraudulent submission based on Tessa Moreno's confirmation that Jordan admitted to fabricating the receipt. [Write action]
Reissue the Canopy Health invoice with a corrected DocNumber using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_canopy" for Canopy Health, doc_number: "INV-2026-0401-R" or next available number, line_items: same line items and amounts as the original $9,210 invoice -- apartment sourcing x3, flights x3, moving coordination x3, stipend platform fee, and pending surcharges). This resolves the duplicate INV-2026-0401 numbering conflict by creating a replacement invoice with a unique DocNumber. [Write action]
Add a comment to the Heartland dispute Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_heartland_q1_recon). The comment should include the current reconciliation status: Heartland conceded $3,200 in credits (Marchetti + Osei), the Venkatesh staging claim for $800 is unresolved pending Chloe's verification of whether Swift also billed for origin staging, the QuickBooks hold remains at $12,800 per Marcus's instruction, and the revised total will be either $8,000 (if Venkatesh staging rejected) or $8,800 (if accepted). [Write action]
Add a comment to the stipend fraud Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). The comment should summarize the resolution: Andre Washington (Mosaic, STIP-2026-0094, $89) confirmed legitimate by client HR -- hold released, Airtable updated to Approved. Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, STIP-2026-0097, $45) confirmed fraudulent by client HR -- written off, plus pending $245 submission also fraudulent (total fraud: $290). Jordan placed on PIP and removed from stipend eligibility by BrightLoop. [Write action]
Add a comment to the DOT compliance ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic or a parent DOT audit ticket). The comment should note: Swift sent the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, closing the critical documentation gap. All four hazmat shipments now have complete documentation. The $50,000 penalty accrual stands pending DOT's formal acknowledgment of the compliance package. 15-day extension through June 5 still awaiting approval. [Write action]
Post the full reconciliation summary in #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005). The message should cover all five open items with their current status:
1. Heartland Q1 Dispute: $3,200 in credits agreed (Marchetti + Osei), $800 Venkatesh staging claim unresolved, QB hold at $12,800 per Marcus, revised total pending Chloe's Swift verification.
2. Swift Fuel Surcharge: $720 bill (SR-2026-FUEL-Q1) already recorded in QuickBooks -- Venkatesh $285, Tran $195, Kovac $240.
3. Stipend Fraud Resolution: Andre Washington $89 hold released (legitimate), Jordan Ekwueme $45 written off (fraudulent, plus $245 pending submission also fake). Airtable records updated.
4. DOT Penalty Accrual: Documentation gap resolved (Swift sent Keiko Tanaka placarding cert), all 4 hazmat shipments compliant. $50K accrual stands pending DOT formal confirmation.
5. Duplicate Invoice (INV-2026-0401): Canopy Health invoice reissued with corrected DocNumber. Mosaic invoice unchanged.
[Write action]
Send the reconciliation email to Marcus with Elena CC'd using send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipient: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The email should contain the full reconciliation covering all five items with current status, actions taken, and items still requiring decision (Heartland Venkatesh staging claim, DOT accrual revision timing, Terraform estimate disposition). The email content should include the specific dollar amounts for each item and flag the open decisions needed before the April month-end close meeting. [Write action]

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

The agent should find account acct_dot_penalty_accrual (Regulatory Compliance -- DOT Penalties, Other Current Liability) with a current balance of $50,000, referencing case PHMSA-2026-04-1187. Given that Chloe's update says the documentation gap is resolved but DOT has not formally confirmed, the agent should note the $50,000 accrual stands for now and flag it as potentially revisable pending DOT's formal response.
Search Airtable stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id for stipend table, query: "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or "TechComfort" or similar). The agent needs to find:
(a) rec_stipend_andre_keyboard -- Andre Washington's $89 ergonomic keyboard, currently "Pending Approval" with $0 approved amount.
(b) rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand -- Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand, currently "Pending Approval" with $0 approved amount.
The agent needs these record IDs to update their statuses based on the client HR verification responses.
Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "stipend" or "DOT" or "invoice" or similar). The agent needs to identify the relevant tracking tickets:
(a) issue_heartland_q1_recon -- "Vendor invoice reconciliation -- Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)" -- the primary Heartland dispute ticket, already has a comment from Marcus dated April 22 with the partial concession details.
(b) issue_stipend_duplicate_detection -- "Stipend duplicate detection -- receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring" -- the stipend fraud investigation ticket tracking the Kevin Tran / Andre Washington / Jordan Ekwueme flagged cluster.
(c) DOT compliance tickets under project_dot_compliance_audit, including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for the Keiko Tanaka placarding certificate gap.
Update the Andre Washington stipend record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: the stipend base ID, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: [{id: "rec_stipend_andre_keyboard", fields: {Approval Status: "Approved", Approved Amount: 89}}]). This releases the administrative hold on the $89 reimbursement based on Tamara Byrd's verification that the purchase is legitimate. [Write action]
Update the Jordan Ekwueme stipend record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: the stipend base ID, table_id: tblStipends00001, records: [{id: "rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand", fields: {Approval Status: "Rejected" or "Written Off" or similar}}]). This writes off the $45 fraudulent submission based on Tessa Moreno's confirmation that Jordan admitted to fabricating the receipt. [Write action]
Reissue the Canopy Health invoice with a corrected DocNumber using quickbooks_create_invoice (customer_ref: "cust_canopy" for Canopy Health, doc_number: "INV-2026-0401-R" or next available number, line_items: same line items and amounts as the original $9,210 invoice -- apartment sourcing x3, flights x3, moving coordination x3, stipend platform fee, and pending surcharges). This resolves the duplicate INV-2026-0401 numbering conflict by creating a replacement invoice with a unique DocNumber. [Write action]
Add a comment to the Heartland dispute Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_heartland_q1_recon). The comment should include the current reconciliation status: Heartland conceded $3,200 in credits (Marchetti + Osei), the Venkatesh staging claim for $800 is unresolved pending Chloe's verification of whether Swift also billed for origin staging, the QuickBooks hold remains at $12,800 per Marcus's instruction, and the revised total will be either $8,000 (if Venkatesh staging rejected) or $8,800 (if accepted). [Write action]
Add a comment to the stipend fraud Linear ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). The comment should summarize the resolution: Andre Washington (Mosaic, STIP-2026-0094, $89) confirmed legitimate by client HR -- hold released, Airtable updated to Approved. Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, STIP-2026-0097, $45) confirmed fraudulent by client HR -- written off, plus pending $245 submission also fraudulent (total fraud: $290). Jordan placed on PIP and removed from stipend eligibility by BrightLoop. [Write action]
Add a comment to the DOT compliance ticket using linear_create_comment (issue_id: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic or a parent DOT audit ticket). The comment should note: Swift sent the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, closing the critical documentation gap. All four hazmat shipments now have complete documentation. The $50,000 penalty accrual stands pending DOT's formal acknowledgment of the compliance package. 15-day extension through June 5 still awaiting approval. [Write action]
Post the full reconciliation summary in #finance using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005). The message should cover all five open items with their current status:
1. Heartland Q1 Dispute: $3,200 in credits agreed (Marchetti + Osei), $800 Venkatesh staging claim unresolved, QB hold at $12,800 per Marcus, revised total pending Chloe's Swift verification.
2. Swift Fuel Surcharge: $720 bill (SR-2026-FUEL-Q1) already recorded in QuickBooks -- Venkatesh $285, Tran $195, Kovac $240.
3. Stipend Fraud Resolution: Andre Washington $89 hold released (legitimate), Jordan Ekwueme $45 written off (fraudulent, plus $245 pending submission also fake). Airtable records updated.
4. DOT Penalty Accrual: Documentation gap resolved (Swift sent Keiko Tanaka placarding cert), all 4 hazmat shipments compliant. $50K accrual stands pending DOT formal confirmation.
5. Duplicate Invoice (INV-2026-0401): Canopy Health invoice reissued with corrected DocNumber. Mosaic invoice unchanged.
[Write action]
Send the reconciliation email to Marcus with Elena CC'd using send_email (sender: hana.kim@moveops.com, recipient: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The email should contain the full reconciliation covering all five items with current status, actions taken, and items still requiring decision (Heartland Venkatesh staging claim, DOT accrual revision timing, Terraform estimate disposition). The email content should include the specific dollar amounts for each item and flag the open decisions needed before the April month-end close meeting. [Write action]
